round4_phase1_complete.methodology: Trades merged to latest BBO at or before trade timestamp (merge_asof backward). Aggressive side: price>=ask1 => buy_aggr; price<=bid1 => sell_aggr. Forward mid change = mid(t+K)-mid(t) on price grid for same symbol (K in ticks 5,20,100) and extract forward in parallel. Stratification: per-Mark role/aggr, per-symbol slices, hour_bin coarse bucket, burst vs control.

bullets.conclusion: Strong positive same-symbol K=5 forward mid after **Mark 67 aggressive buys** (n~164, t~13) and after **Mark 22 as seller on others' aggressive buys** (n~109, t~9.5); day-split tables show **positive mean all three days** for Mark67 buy_aggr K5 and Mark22-as-seller buy_aggr K5. Mark 22 **sell_aggr** rows show **negative** short-horizon same-symbol drift (n large). Mark 01 on VEV_5300 (either side) shows **slightly negative** K5 in filtered slice — not a buy tail.

bullets.bullet: 2) Deviation from bot baseline (buyer,seller,symbol cell means)
bullets.conclusion: Cell-mean baseline for fwd20 leaves structured residuals in thin cells; top file lists largest |mean residual| pairs — use for second-order conditioning after more n.
Dominant edge Mark01→Mark22 (1339 rows) matches repo sketch; hydro Mark14↔38 / 38→14 duopoly; two-hop file lists frequent middlemen chains.
318 burst timestamps vs 800 random-trade controls: **mean extract forward-20 mid ~0.30 vs ~0.34** — no burst premium on extract in this slice (null / negative for burst thesis).

bullets.bullet: 5) Adverse selection proxy (aggressive buy markout by seller, K20 same symbol)
bullets.conclusion: Aggressive buys against **Mark 22** show highest mean fwd20 (~2.0 on n~109); against Mark 01 ~flat — supports **not** treating Mark22 as uninformed passive on short hold after lift.

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: Follow / piggyback **Mark 67 aggressive buys** (buy_aggr): mean same-symbol K5 fwd mid ~+1.96 (n~164), stable positive **days 1–3** (means ~1.75–2.2, frac_pos 0.73–0.90). Effect: ~2 ticks / 5 steps on printed symbol.
top_5_tradeable_edges.caveat: Tradeable only if sim can enter same direction with realistic fees; needs backtest trader, not tape-only.

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: When **Mark 22 is seller** on an **aggressive buy** (others lift), same-symbol **K5** fwd mid ~+2.07 (n~109), stable **days 1–3**.
top_5_tradeable_edges.caveat: Confound: may be basket/ladder prints (Mark01→22); condition on symbol and burst.

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: **Fade / avoid supplying liquidity** into paths where **Mark 22 sell_aggr** dominates short horizon: negative mean K5 same-symbol (large n).
top_5_tradeable_edges.caveat: Implementation = MM skew or pull quotes when Mark22 hits ask aggressively.

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: **Mark 49** as seller on aggressive buys: positive K5/K20 means in smaller n (~104) — secondary candidate for conditioning.
top_5_tradeable_edges.caveat: Lower count; verify in trader sim.

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: Graph-structural: **Mark01→Mark22** concentration + hydro **14↔38** loop — use as **regime / schedule** features for multi-leg bots (not a scalar markout).
top_5_tradeable_edges.caveat: Edge is routing / timing, not a single-product scalar.

no_edge_findings: Burst timestamps (n≥3 prints) do **not** outperform random timestamps on **extract** 20-tick forward mid (means ~0.30 vs ~0.34; see r4_p1_bursts_eventstudy.txt).
Mark 01 involvement on **VEV_5300** small-sample aggregate shows **negative** K5 in the either-side filter (n~132) — do not blindly chase 01 flow on 5300 without re-stratifying by aggr side.
Hour-bin stratification file is coarse (timestamp scaling); treat as exploratory only.

analysis_description: Round 4 `trader_v22.py` is an intentional **no-op** baseline: `TradingState` in `Trader.run` does not include tape counterparty fields, so Phase 1 evidence is produced **offline** from `Prosperity4Data/ROUND_4/trades_*.csv` (see `r4_phase1_counterparty.py`). v22 backtest (round 4, days 1-3) confirms zero PnL with worse+all.
used_to_decide: Phase 2 should inject precomputed Mark signals (from outputs/*.csv) into traderData or extend test_runner to pass last-tape trades.

round4_phase2_complete.interaction_with_phase1: Phase 1 Mark67 / Mark22-as-seller positive short-horizon **mid** markouts are **not** trivially monetizable: `trader_v23.py` (lift V5300 after Mark67 aggressive extract buys, merged-timeline triggers from tape) loses **-16,879** total R4 days 1-3 under worse and all (same). **Refines** Phase 1: tape predictability does not survive **worse** touch execution on V5300 with naive window+flatten. Burst Mark01→22 multi-VEV: mean V5300 fwd20 ~+0.34 (n=287, frac_pos 0.52) — **weak** vs Phase 1 null on extract bursts; orchestrator mode buyer Mark01 on 286/287 bursts (see `outputs/r4_p2_burst_orchestrator.csv`).

bullets.conclusion: Mark01→22 VEV-only bursts (n=287): mean V5300 fwd20 ~+0.34, median +0.5, ~52% positive — modest, not a clean fade/trend split; orchestrator almost always Mark 01.
Extract: tight spread (<=2) shows **positive** mean(micro-mid) ~+0.41; wider <=6 bucket near zero mean — inclineGod-style spread object differs from mid in tight books.
Per-timestamp signed flow: corr(VEV_5300, EXTRACT) near **0** at lags 0–10 — no simple lead-lag scalars in this aggregation.

bullets.bullet: 4) Regime splits (implicit via spread buckets in microprice table)
bullets.conclusion: Signal concentrated in **tight** extract spread regime for microprice bias.

bullets.bullet: 5) Vol / smile (IV residual vs Mark01 density, coarse subsample)
bullets.conclusion: Subsampling timestamps: IV(5300) vs median IV strip vs Mark01 event count — exploratory CSV for Phase 3 joint-gate overlays.
Naive **follow Mark67** on V5300 is **strongly negative** under worse+all; supports **not** lifting touch without richer microstructure / Phase 3 Sonic gate.
Not implemented this pass (v23 uses flat outside window only); Phase 3 may combine gate + skew.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.edge: Microprice vs mid **bias in tight extract spread** (<=2) — use as **signal object** (inclineGod), not as naive counterparty chase.
Burst **Mark01→22** VEV ladder: weak positive V5300 fwd20 mean — candidate **event** feature for scheduling detection, not standalone PnL.
**Falsified in sim:** Phase-1-style **follow Mark67** long V5300 touch → large loss (confirms Phase1 caveat mid≠PnL).
Signed-flow lag structure ~null → avoid Granger-style V5300→extract linear rules without richer state.
IV residual file seeds **Mark01 × smile** interaction tests under Sonic gate (Phase 3).

analysis_description: Phase 2 script `r4_phase2_analysis.py` + `trader_v23.py` (Mark67-triggered V5300) + burst orchestrator CSV. Phase 2 completion object `round4_phase2_complete` appended.
used_to_decide: Proceed to Phase 3: re-run highest-value slices **conditional on Sonic joint 5200+5300 spread gate** using paths under `round3work/vouchers_final_strategy` (moved to `previous round work/` if present in tree).

round4_phase3_complete.joint_gate_definition: At each tape row: spread(symbol)=ask1-bid1; joint_tight iff VEV_5200 and VEV_5300 spreads both <= 2 (same TH as Round 3 Sonic note).

tape_recheck_mark67_extract.slice: Mark 67 aggressive buy on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT (price>=ask1); forward extract mid K=5 ticks on price grid
tape_recheck_mark67_extract.finding: Joint-tight subset does **not** show higher mean fwd5 than non-tight on R4 days 1-3 (n_tight=27 mean~1.78 vs n_loose=137 mean~2.00; frac_pos similar). Sonic-style **per-event doubling** of mid-edge is **not** reproduced on this counterparty slice — gate still useful for **execution risk** (fewer triggers) not for **stronger** mid continuation here.

spread_spread_correlations.finding: Extract spread vs 5200/5300 spreads ~uncorrelated (~0.01); 5200 vs 5300 spread corr ~0.31 (co-movement of voucher book widths).

sim_trader.vs_v23: Same precomputed Mark67 extract triggers; v24 adds joint gate before lifting V5300. R4 days 1-3 total PnL improves from -16,879 (v23) to -5,883 (v24) under worse and all — still negative, smaller adverse footprint.

followup_v5300_trade_prints.slice: All VEV_5300 tape trades R4 days 1-3; K = forward price-grid rows. Joint tight = 5200&5300 spread both <=2 at print ts.
followup_v5300_trade_prints.finding: R4 has only **~165** V5300 trade rows across three days; **163/165** are already joint-tight (only **1** loose with valid mids). Tight: mean same-symbol fwd K5 **-0.19** (sell-aggr heavy); K100 **+0.26**. Confirms: **Sonic book state is the default** whenever 5300 prints — the gate stratifies almost nothing in aggregate tape stats; PnL edge instead comes from **Who + when** (Mark67+window sim), not from conditioning raw 5300 prints on gate here.

tier_a_sim_update.interaction: (Mark67 prefilter + Sonic joint at lift + window exit) x **Q=5** x **FLATTEN_MAX=9** sell clip
tier_a_sim_update.note: Flatten grid extended below 10: **v75 FLATTEN=9 +3722** > v74=8 +3703 > v70=11 +3699 > v69=10 +3693; **v73=7** +3566 (day_1 crushed). **W≠50k** with Q=5 stays bad — keep JSON **50_000**. Tape: `r4_phase13` — mean **~43%** of price rows inside each v26 window have joint gate tight (execution often off-tight surface).

analysis_description: Phase 3 script fix: R4 price CSV has no `spread` column — compute extract spread from ask1-bid1. Ran joint-gate Mark67 extract fwd5 table + spread-spread correlations; shipped `trader_v24.py` (Sonic gate + Mark67) with worse+all backtests.
used_to_decide: Tape: joint gate does not strengthen Mark67 extract fwd-mid on R4; keep gate for sim hygiene / fewer bad lifts. Next iterations could gate on extract spread or fade when joint gate breaks mid-position.

analysis_description: Post–Phase 3 extension: **Mark 22 as seller on aggressive buys** (Phase 1 rank-2 slice). Script `r4_phase4_m22_joint_gate.py` merges trades to BBO, flags buy_aggr, joins Sonic joint gate at trade timestamp, measures **same-symbol** forward mid K=5 on R4 days 1–3. Emits `r4_v25_signals.json` for sim.
used_to_decide: Tape: pooled n=109 mean fwd5 **~2.07** ticks, **~85%** positive — matches Phase 1 direction. **Joint tight** subset n=29 has **lower** mean (**~1.64**) than non-tight (**~2.23**); for **extract-only** Mark22 prints (n=90), tight n=18 mean **~1.61** vs loose n=72 mean **~2.13** — Sonic gate is **not** a positive filter for this counterparty mix (unlike the community story for a different signal). Sim `trader_v25.py` (lift extract after triggers + joint gate) still loses **-19,005** days 1–3 worse+all — **mid markout does not monetize** with touch in/out + window flatten; do not deploy naive piggyback.

analysis_description: Tape refinement `r4_phase5_mark67_micro_joint.py`: Mark67 aggressive extract prints (n=164) vs **joint tight at print** (n=27). Forward **VEV_5300** mid: pooled meanK5 **0.034** / fracpos **0.29**; joint-tight subset meanK5 **0.056** / fracpos **0.33**, meanK20 **0.037** / fracpos **0.44**. Documented that **micro>mid** is **164/164** on this slice (redundant with aggressive-at-ask). Wrote `r4_v26_signals.json` (27 merged timestamps).
used_to_decide: `trader_v26.py` uses **only** joint-tight-at-print Mark67 triggers then the same v24 execution gate. R4 days 1–3 sim turns **positive** (**+768** worse+all) vs **-5,883** for v24 — fewer, better-aligned windows reduce adverse selection, though day_3 still negative and edge is small vs transaction risk.

analysis_description: Execution experiment `trader_v27.py`: identical signals to v26 but **sell to bid whenever joint gate opens** (5200 or 5300 spread >2) while still inside the Mark67 window — intended as Sonic-style regime exit.
used_to_decide: **Falsified in sim** for this naive rule: total **-21,200** worse+all vs **+768** v26 — forced exits on transient widening likely hit **worse** bid prints and forfeit continuation that v26 captured by holding until window end. Keep v26-style exit (window-only) unless a smarter unwind (passive join bid / inventory cap) is tested.

analysis_description: Tape: `r4_phase6_joint_persistence.py` — after each **v26** trigger, first price-grid step (100) where joint **not** tight: **median offset 100**, mean **274**, mean sampled fraction of window still tight **~0.43** (see `outputs/r4_p6_joint_tight_persistence_after_v26_triggers.txt`). Explains v27 churn (gate opens quickly) but also shows halving sim window is not automatically optimal.
used_to_decide: `trader_v28.py` (25k window) total **-1,823** worse+all vs **+768** v26 — shorter window cuts profitable day-2 carry despite tape showing joint gate often flickers open soon after print.

analysis_description: Parameter tweak `trader_v29.py`: same as v26 but lift clip **Q=4** (vs 6) for adverse-selection control.
used_to_decide: Total **+377** worse+all vs **+768** v26 — smaller clip trims day-3 loss slightly but sacrifices day-2 notional capture; **keep v26** as primary variant unless multi-day grid expands.

analysis_description: Tape `r4_phase7_extract_spread_at_v26.py`: at each **v26** trigger, extract BBO spread at print — **27/27** already **<=2** (mean ~1.59); forward V5300 stats match joint-tight subset from phase5. So print-time extract filter is redundant; **execution-time** extract<=2 is a different test.
used_to_decide: `trader_v30.py` adds extract spread<=2 **when lifting** — total **-3** worse+all vs **+768** v26: joint-tight windows often coincide with **wider** extract book later, so the extra gate **removes the profitable lifts** and leaves near-flat noise.

analysis_description: `trader_v31.py`: v26 with V5300 spread cap **4** instead of **6**.
used_to_decide: PnL **identical** to v26 (**+768** worse+all) — in practice lifts occur only when V5300 spread already **<=4** in this trigger set; tighter cap is a no-op here.

analysis_description: Merged-timeline **v26 window overlap** (`r4_phase8_v26_window_overlap.py`): **max_simultaneous_active_windows=3** (W=50_000), merged union length **1,197,819** vs raw sum **1,350,027** — material overlap. Triggers per day (merged): day_1 **5**, day_2 **7**, day_3 **15**.
used_to_decide: `trader_v32.py` caps V5300 long at **18** during windows (3x clip 6). Total **+211** worse+all vs **+768** v26 — cap **cuts day-2 upside** more than it fixes day_3; overlapping windows were helping notional on the winning leg. **Keep v26** unless inventory risk mandates a higher cap than 18 or a per-window reset rule.

analysis_description: Tape drill: **merged day 3** v26 triggers only (`r4_phase9_v26_day3_context.py`). **n=15**; mean gap (merged start-to-start) **~61k**; forward **VEV_5300** mid at print: meanK5 **-0.033**, fracposK5 **0.33**, meanK20 **0**, fracposK20 **~0.47** — weak short-horizon edge vs days 1-2 in phase5 table, consistent with sim day_3 drag.
used_to_decide: `trader_v33.py` (**MAX_Q=36**) total **+532** worse+all vs **+768** v26 — softer cap than v32 recovers some day-2 but still below uncapped v26; day_3 loss **-1044** vs **-6697** v26 (smaller inventory → smaller loss magnitude).

analysis_description: `trader_v34.py`: **MAX_Q=24** (between v32 and v33).
used_to_decide: Total **+288** worse+all. Among capped variants on this three-day run: **v26 uncapped 768** > **v33 (cap 36) 532** > **v29 (Q=4) 377** > **v34 (cap 24) 288** > **v32 (cap 18) 211**. Higher cap tracks closer to v26 but remains below it; none beat uncapped v26.

analysis_description: `trader_v35.py`: intended **skip new lifts on tape day 3** using `state.timestamp >= 2_000_000` — **incorrect**: `TradingState.timestamp` in `TestRunner` is **per-day local** (same 0..max as CSV), **not** merged-timeline absolute. So the guard never activated; v35 matched v26 by accident.
used_to_decide: For calendar-style rules in live `Trader.run`, either pass **day index** via `traderData` from a wrapper, or **pre-filter triggers** in JSON (see v36). Do not assume merged offsets in `state.timestamp` without verifying the runner.

analysis_description: `r4_phase10_window_cross_day3.py`: **zero** v26 triggers satisfy `T < 2e6 <= T+W` — day-2 triggers end **before** merged day 3 (`max(day2 T)+W = 1_922_400 < 2e6`). `r4_phase11_v36_signals.json` drops **15** triggers with `T >= 2e6` (tape day 3 prints), leaving **12**. `trader_v36.py` uses that file.
used_to_decide: v36 PnL still **+768** identical to v26 — removing day-3-origin triggers does **not** change day_3 **-6697**; that loss is **unwind of inventory** from day 1–2 windows (and/or overlapping activity), not from the 15 dropped triggers alone. True day-3 mitigation needs **flatten-before-roll**, **shorter W**, or **caps** (already explored).

analysis_description: **Critical sim bugfix:** precomputed merged trigger lists were compared to **per-day local** `state.timestamp` without adding `day_cum_offset[day]`, so post-trigger windows **never** extended across tape-day boundaries (e.g. day-2 trigger invisible on day-3 local clock). Patched `TradingState.day_num` + `merged_ts_util.window_active` and updated all R4 merged-signal traders (v23–v36, v25). Re-backtest `trader_v26.py`: total **+3,231** worse+all (day3 **+1,686** vs prior **-6,697**). Prior `results.json` rows for v26-class runs used the buggy matcher.
used_to_decide: `trader_v37.py` adds **EOD flatten** on tape days 1–2 (local ts >= 994_900) before roll; total **+3,101** — slightly below fixed v26 due to early exit cost on day_1 (**373** vs **503**).

analysis_description: **EOD PAD sweep** on fixed v26: `trader_v38.py` uses PAD=**500** (last 500 local ts before 999_900) on days 1–2 — total **+3,253** worse+all (**+22** vs fixed v26 **+3,231**), day_1 **+525** vs **+503**. `trader_v39.py` EOD **day 2 only** same PAD: **identical +3,231** to v26 (day-2 end flatten redundant vs window exit for this tape).
used_to_decide: Ship **v38** as best EOD-carry tweak so far; keep v26 baseline for minimalism.

analysis_description: **Post-fix reruns** for earlier Mark67 strategies (merged `window_active`): **v23** **-8,208** (full trigger list, still bad); **v24** **-625** (Sonic gate helps vs v23 but negative); **v27** **-46,438** (gate-break exit catastrophic once cross-day windows actually fire). Logs `trader_v{23,24,27}_2026-04-26_r4_*_mergedfix.log`.
used_to_decide: Pre-filtered **v26/v38** line remains the only positive Mark67+V5300 branch in this sweep; do not resurrect v27-style forced exits.

analysis_description: **EOD_PAD grid** on fixed v26+v38 logic (tape days 1–2 flatten before roll): 200/300 → **+3,231** (same as bare v26); 500 (v38) **+3,253**; 600 **+3,273**; **750 (v42) +3,279**; 1000 **+3,218** (worse: early exit costs day_1). Effect is **not** monotonic; best is a **~750** local-timestamp pre-close band.
used_to_decide: Ship **trader_v42** (+26 vs fixed v26, +26 vs v38) as the tuned EOD variant; v38 (500) remains a simpler near-best if you want one knob.

analysis_description: **EOD fine sweep** 700-900 + 780/820: **PAD=800 and 820** tie at **+3,284** (day_1 556) vs v42(750) **+3,279**; 700/750 match 3279; 900 at **+3,254**. Flat optimum band **~800-820** local ts before 999_900. `trader_v46` / `v49` = best EOD; `r4_phase12`: all **VEV_5300** tape prints n=165 with **163 joint-tight** (gate rarely discriminates on raw prints in R4).
used_to_decide: Ship **v46** or **v49** (identical PnL) as strict best EOD; update default from v42. Phase 3 object extended with `followup_v5300_trade_prints` (joint gate is almost always on when 5300 trades in this R4 sample).

analysis_description: Execution sweep on **v46** (Mark67+Sonic+EOD800): **Q** ∈ {4,5,6,7}, **MAX_SPREAD_V** ∈ {4,6,8}, **EOD_PAD** 810, and **Q=5** with EOD 700/780/820. **Q=5** (`trader_v50`) total **+3,470** vs v46 **+3,284** — gain is **+283 day_3** and **+70 day_1** with **-167 day_2** vs Q=6; smaller inventory dominates. **Q=7** **+3,207** (day_3 collapses). **Q=4** **+3,183**. **MAX_SPREAD_V** 4/8 identical to 6 (no-op). With **Q=5**, EOD_PAD 700–820 all **identical +3,470** — EOD flatten rarely fires before window exit when clip caps position.
used_to_decide: Tier-A sim candidate: **Mark67 filtered + Sonic exec + lift Q=5** (`v50`/`v56`/`v57`/`v58` equivalent). Prefer **v50** (EOD=800 matches prior tuned band). Keep v46/v49 if you insist on Q=6 for notional.

analysis_description: **Post-v50 sweep:** (1) **Post-trigger W** 40k/45k/55k/60k with Q=5 — **40k catastrophic** (day_2 **-3769**), 45k/55k/60k all **below** W=50k baseline; **keep 50k from JSON**. (2) **FLATTEN_MAX** (per sell to bid at window end + EOD): coarse 18→3586, 15→3616, 12→3667; fine **10–14** peaks at **11** (**v70 +3699**), 10 +3693, 12 +3667, 13 +3651, 14 +3615. Smaller clips **slow** unwind → **more favorable** bid fills on this tape. (3) **v63–v68** logged in `results.json` iter 47–52.
used_to_decide: Do not shrink **W** off 50k without re-validation. Flatten grid **10–30** logged in **`results.json`** iters **47–56** (v63–v72). **Superseded** by iter **24** (FLATTEN **7–9** + Q cross-check → **v75** best).

analysis_description: **Phase 13 tape:** `r4_phase13_joint_tight_in_v26_windows.py` — among **price-grid** timestamps inside each Mark67+v26 **50k** window, fraction where **5200&5300** spreads both **<=2**: mean **~0.43**, median **~0.46**, min **~0.02**, max **~0.83** (`outputs/r4_p13_joint_tight_fraction_per_v26_window.csv`). Confirms joint-tight is **intermittent** during the sim hold window (not a constant Sonic surface). **Sim bracket** below FLATTEN=10: **v75 (9) +3,722** best; v74 (8) +3,703; v73 (7) +3,566; **Q×flatten**: v76 Q=4 +3,454; v77 Q=6 +3,454 with FLATTEN=11.
used_to_decide: Default sim candidate → **`trader_v75.py`**; keep **v70** as near-best if you want a round-number flatten cap.
